blurb: The wetland monarch host: flat-topped domes of small rose-pink stars over slender willow-like leaves, humming with butterflies and bees. Unlike its dryland cousins, it wants consistently moist ground.
water: High — moist to wet soil; not for dry zones
spread: Clumping; expands slowly, far less aggressive than showy milkweed
seasons: Slender willow-like leaves on tall stems; rose-pink flower domes mid-to-late summer; slim seed pods splitting to silky floss in fall
wildlife: Monarch host (caterpillar food) and a major nectar source for butterflies, bees and other pollinators
deer: Generally avoided — the foliage is toxic
toxic: All parts contain toxic cardiac glycosides and irritant milky latex; poisonous raw to livestock, pets and people — see Edible parts
